notes: The 'Quite OK Image' format by Dominic Szablewski (phoboslab), released 2021 — a ~300-line lossless image codec. The widely-held reading is 'koy', identical to the ornamental koi fish: the official logo stylizes the Q into a fish, making the pun explicit (no formal written pronunciation note from the creator exists, so it's inferred from the logo and community usage). Rhymes with 'boy'. Despite this, many developers spell it out letter-by-letter as 'kew-oh-EYE' (Q-O-I) because it reads like a standard acronym. The sibling audio format QOA ('Quite OK Audio') follows the same koi-style joke.
